meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: Space Force awards TrustPoint $4 million for LEO navigation demonstration
immediate.summary: The startup is developing C-band system as alternative to GPS

immediate.title: Anduril wins Army contract for prototype C2 missile defense system
immediate.summary: The system will use Anduril’s Lattice software, where it will allow the Army to collect and combine information from separate, existing missile defense systems and transform that data into a comprehensive operating picture

immediate.title: ‘Doesn’t Happen Without These 6 Airmen’: F-16 Pilots Awarded DFCs for Midnight Hammer
immediate.summary: A half-dozen pilots from one of the Air Force’s premier F-16 units, the 20th Fighter Wing at Shaw Air Force Base, S.C., have been awarded one of the service’s highest decorations for their role in Operation Midnight Hammer, the June 2025 strikes against Iranian nuclear facilities. The post Doesn’t Happen Without These 6 Airmen: F-16 Pilots Awarded DFCs for Midnight Hammer appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Air Force Rescue Units Awarded PUCs for Afghanistan Evacuation
immediate.summary: Two Air Force rescue squadrons recently received the the Presidential Unit Citation award for heroism displayed during the evacuation of Afghanistan, according to a May 10 announcement from Air Combat Command. The post Air Force Rescue Units Awarded PUCs for Afghanistan Evacuation appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Space Force to Overhaul Key Early Warning, Surveillance Radars Around the World
immediate.summary: The Space Force plans to overhaul eight legacy missile warning and space surveillance radars located around the world, taking them from analog to digital operations, according to a May 7 notice. The post Space Force to Overhaul Key Early Warning, Surveillance Radars Around the World appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Pentagon Relents, Seeks Funds for E-7 Wedgetail
immediate.summary: The Pentagon has now committed to funding the E-7A Wedgetail in its 2027 budget request after previously zeroing out its spending. The post Pentagon Relents, Seeks Funds for E-7 Wedgetail appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

Hello Universe: NASA’s Next-Gen Space Processor Undergoes Testing
immediate.summary: NASA’s High Performance Spaceflight Computing project aims to dramatically improve the computing power of spacecraft. Missions need processors that can withstand the harsh space environment, so they use chips developed years ago that are hardy and reliable. But upgraded chips are needed to enable the development of autonomous spacecraft, accelerate the rate of scientific discovery []

Harmon serves as the Artemis II mission interface manager for NASA’s Deep Space Network, an []

immediate.title: Space Force Lays Out How Officers Can Advance Their Careers
immediate.summary: Guardian officers looking to advance their careers must master their job specialty but also have a strong all-around understanding of the service and the joint force, the Space Force detailed in a new officer career path document. The post Space Force Lays Out How Officers Can Advance Their Careers appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Transcelestial tests space-to-ground laser communications technologies
immediate.summary: Transcelestial has advanced its work to provide space-to-ground optical communications with a test of laser transmissions from a satellite to ground stations.

immediate.title: Space is having its internet moment. Investors should act accordingly.
immediate.summary: In the 1990s, the internet stopped being a novelty and started to resemble what it is today. The release of the Mosaic browser in 1993 made the web usable for ordinary people. Amazon listed on Nasdaq in 1997. And by 2000, Cisco briefly became the most valuable company in the world. The bubble later burst […]

NASA retaining six-month ISS missions
immediate.summary: NASA plans to continue exchanging International Space Station crews about every six months after considering longer stays.

immediate.title: Nicholas Houghton: Engineering Crew Safety for NASA’s Artemis Missions
immediate.summary: Nicholas Houghton always dreamed of working at NASA and one day becoming an astronaut. Today, he helps design systems that keep crews safe during missions aboard NASA’s Orion spacecraft, including the successful Artemis II mission around the Moon. After joining NASA as a Pathways intern, Houghtonlaterbecamea full-time engineer on the Orion Crew Survival Systems (OCSS) []

immediate.title: Air Force Reserve Wants to Put 24 F-35s at Homestead
immediate.summary: The Air Force wants to replace 24 F-16 Fighting Falcons at Homestead Air Reserve Base in Florida with an equal number of F-35A Joint Strike Fighters. The post Air Force Reserve Wants to Put 24 F-35s at Homestead appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

Links to view the Lunaboticscompetition live can be found on the agencys Lunabotics page. The competition is slated to run between 8 a.m. and 6 p.m. each day. Media are invited to attendthe []

immediate.title: Navy unveils three new PAEs for aviation, mission systems, munitions
immediate.summary: “The establishment of these PAEs today will accelerate acquisition efforts in three key portfolios,” said Jason Potter, who is performing the duties of assistant secretary of the Navy for research and development.

immediate.title: Failing to pass a defense budget is a self-inflicted wound in the space race
immediate.summary: As we have learned from the current conflicts in Ukraine and Iran, space-enabled capability has become critical to all we do in the military. It is persistent (always there) and operates in denied areas (first in the battlefield). These capabilities provide critical combat effects: intelligence, missile threat warning, rapid communications and necessary and amazingly accurate […]

immediate.summary: The increase from $25 billion just two weeks ago reflects “updated repair and replacement of equipment costs,” along with the “general operational costs."

immediate.title: Star Catcher raises $65 million for space power grid
immediate.summary: Star Catcher Industries, a company developing power-beaming technology for satellites, has raised $65 million to validate the technology in space.

immediate.summary: Star Catcher Industries just raised $65 million to build a constellation of power-beaming satellites, which the Florida company thinks will supercharge the off-Earth economy.

same_day.title: Creotech plans $118 million capital raise, investment in new satellite factory
same_day.summary: WARSAW, Poland — Polish space technology company Creotech Instruments has announced plans for a $118 million fundraise that will allow the company to open a new satellite production facility in Poland by 2029 as part of a new long term development strategy. Creotech Instruments hopes to quadruple its manufacturing capacities to around 40 satellites annually […]

same_day.title: Cowboy raises $275 million to build rockets with orbital data center upper stages
same_day.summary: Cowboy Space, founded less than two years ago as Aetherflux to develop space-based solar power, has raised $275 million at a $2 billion valuation to build rockets with upper stages that would serve as data centers once in low Earth orbit.

same_day.summary: Description NASA’s Curiosity Mars rover used its Mast Camera, or Mastcam, to capture this view of a rock nicknamed “Atacama” on May 6, 2026, the 4,877th Martian day, or sol, of the mission. The rock had gotten stuck to the drill on the end of Curiosity’s robotic arm on April 25. Engineers spent several days […]

same_day.title: China's Tianzhou 10 freighter delivers 7 tons of cargo to Tiangong space station
same_day.summary: The Tianzhou 10 freighter delivered nearly seven tons of supplies to China's Tiangong space station early Monday morning (May 10), about five hours after launching atop a Long March 7 rocket.

same_day.title: How NASA lunar scientists taught Artemis 2 astronauts to see the moon with different eyes
same_day.summary: The astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis 2 mission that flew around the moon had to learn to describe their observations using language that would help lunar scientists gain a deeper understanding of the lunar environment. What they saw was a surprise to everyone.

same_day.title: SpaceX launches secret US spy satellites to orbit from California (video, photos)
same_day.summary: SpaceX launched a batch of spy satellites for the U.S. government this evening (May 11) from California's foggy central coast.

same_day.title: New James Webb Space Telescope images reveal how massive star clusters can reshape galaxies
same_day.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has helped scientists peek into the secrets of galactic evolution — and the view is stunning, too.

same_day.title: Raytheon awarded SeaRAM contract for Australian frigates
same_day.summary: The contract covers the three Aussie frigates that will be built in Japan.

same_day.title: Simulation software models aerial firefighting dynamics in real time
same_day.summary: Researchers at the University of Glasgow have developed flight simulation software capable of modeling the complex interactions between helicopter rotors, wildfire behavior and water drops in real time, in what they describe as a first for aerial firefighting training. The prototype software, developed as part of the Daedalus I flight simulation framework, runs on consumer-grade GPU hardware and is designed to replace the pre-computed environmental models typically used in conventional flight simulators. The work is published in the CEAS Aeronautical Journal. Continue reading Simulation software models aerial firefighting dynamics in real time at Aerospace Testing International.

same_day.title: Tianzhou-10 cargo spacecraft arrives at Tiangong space station
same_day.summary: China launched the Tianzhou-10 cargo spacecraft late Sunday, docking with the Tiangong space station hours later to deliver supplies, equipment, experiments and propellant.

same_day.title: Tech stocks today: Semiconductor stocks sell off, Sam Altman to take stand in OpenAI v. Musk trial
U.S. Air Force Plans to Deploy Cruise Missiles from Cargo Aircraft by 2027
same_day.summary: The Rapid Dragon program, now called Dragon Cart, transitioned to a Program of Record and will see cargo aircraft able to deploy palletized cruise missiles by 2027. The U.S. Air Force’s Rapid Dragon program is now an official Program of Record (PoR), the Air Force Life Cycle Management Center (AFLCMC) announced on Apr. 30, 2026. []

same_day.title: ESA and JAXA finalize agreement on Apophis asteroid mission
same_day.summary: ESA and JAXA have finalized an agreement to collaborate on a mission to study the asteroid Apophis during its close flyby of Earth in 2029.

same_day.title: MDA Space continues work on Gateway robotic arm
same_day.summary: MDA Space is continuing work on a robotic arm for the lunar Gateway while it discusses the future of the project with the Canadian Space Agency.

same_day.summary: A small silhouette of a High-speed Anti-Radiation Missile can be seen painted below the cockpit in this new photo released by CENTCOM, indicating that the jet was likely responsible for destroying an Iranian air defence site. The small but clear marking almost certainly denotes a direct hit against an Iranian air defence radar site or []

watch.title: Taiwan’s parliament passes pared-back supplementary defense budget
